Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine, mild hyperkalemia), marked hyperbilirubinemia, mild hyperchloremia, metabolic acidosis (low pH, low O2 saturation), and dyslipidemia (high total and LDL cholesterol). Liver enzymes are normal. Hematology and coagulation are within normal limits. Findings suggest acute or chronic kidney injury with possible secondary metabolic and respiratory disturbances; urgent clinical evaluation is recommended.
